entry.title: Re: Styling Total in SAC table
entry.content:
Hi SAC lovers,

I'm not sure if the behavior changed with one of the constant updates, but I successfully formatted my total lines (only). To give you an example / instructions: 

  1. I have a table with dimensions in rows called (1) "Solution Area" and (2) "Sub-Solution Area" and a measure column.
  2. I've turned on "Show totals" on both dimensions.
  3. I've clicked the first cell showing a Totals number in the Sub-Solution Area created a basic Style (blue font color, light grey cell background, alignment right) named "SubSol Style"
  4. I've kept the 'cursor' in that cell and created a Styling Rule "SubSol StylingRule" with
    1. "Data" and "Header" boxes checked for dim Sub-Solution Area
    2. Level = "Self & Descendants"
    3. "Data" and "Header" boxes unchecked for dim Solution Area and Measures
    4. Chose Style "SubSol Style" 

See the result in the attached picture.

Good luck & Best Regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: Inplace upgrade to SAP Data Services 4.3
entry.content:
Inplace upgrade of data services is possible in RHEL 8.10 environment.

Please make sure the versions are compatible from the documentation-  Data Services PAM 4.3 and IPS and DS compatibility matrix

Here is the in-place upgrade documentation- Upgrade scenarios | SAP Help Portal 

To download the software, you can go to this link- Software Center - SAP for Me > support pack and patches >By Alphabetical Index (A-Z) > 'D' >SAP DATA SERVICES > SAP DATA SERVICES 4.3 and find the IPS , DS and DS Client tool versions you are looking to download.

##1388363 - Where can I download Data Services and Information Steward software, support packages, and patches?

Thanks

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to configure the core business processes of SAP S/4HANA Service?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Performing Basic Customizing for Service in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the basic configuration settings required for thel business processes of SAP S/4HANA Service and the integration with other functional areas in S/4HANA like Logistics and also Controlling. This learning journey is valid for anyone with process level knowledge of SAP S/4HANA Service and that now wants to learn how to configure it. The release of SAP S/4HANA that is used is on premise (OP) or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, business users and application consultants will be able to configure the core business processes of SAP S/4HANA Service. They will also gain insights about the configuration of the integration with Logistics, Controlling and the Cross-Application Time Sheet (CATS).

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Create Interval Allowed Characteristic Value
entry.content:
Hi,

I am facing similar problem in a LSMW for creating batch characteristics. LSMW uses BAPI method using BO BUS1088 and CREATE method. However I input E1BPCHARACTVALUESNUM, it always says "

An error has occurred assigning data (E1BPCHARACTVALUESNUM)"
Could you please help me on this.

Thank you,

Priya

"

entry.title: Re: Posting Journal Entries with Tax Using SOAP Posting APIs
entry.content:
Hi Alice,

oh ok - I'm bit a disappointed that is the behaviour.

We even tried changing the settings for FF-707 via OBA5 for online and batch, but still the error is not invoked and incorrect tax calculations are allowed to be posted.

Is there any product support contact I can petition for this to be part of the API error checks?

Thanks

Steve

entry.title: Re: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Journey to Clean Core with SAP MaxAttention and RISE with SAP' (July 16, 2
entry.content:
@manika_99 Hello, thank you for your comment. The link to the recording of the workshop will be published in this exact blog post within the next couple of days.

Thank you and best regards,

Paul Schropp

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE for MARA Characteristic value not replaced
entry.content: One workaround is to delete the classification allocation with BAPI_OBJCL_DELETE and then use the BAPI_OBJCL_CREATE function to create a new allocation.

entry.title: SAP Marketing Cloud top interest in segmentation
entry.content:
Dear experts,

does anybody have an idea of how to determine the "top interest" of a contact in segmentation?

Background/Use Case: 

A contact has multiple interactions with different interests assigned, e.g.

  • 10 interactions with interest A
  • 7 interactions with interest B
  • 20 interactions with interest C

Marketing wants to send out an email based on the "top interest" of the contact. So the contact should only receive one email (not three). In our case the email with the content for interest C.

Using segmentation we could only determine which contacts have which interest items and could use "Count of Interactions". But that would not solve our requirement.

Happy for any ideas.

Kind Regards

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Can we use AMDP in our projects?

entry.title: Re: Can we use AMDP in our projects?

entry.title: Re: Migration within Rise Program

entry.title: Carry Forward - deletes the first opening month - SAC
entry.content:
Hey 
I have a very simple carry forward that doesn't work.
I use a flow dimension with - Opening, changes and closing. 
The opening balance is already copied from last actual to January 2025
Then changes planned are manually inserted on 2025 (changes are named "plug number" in my case). 
My goal is to calculate the closing. 
It looks like I did something wrong in my formula (below) because the Jan2025 opening is deleted, and the carryforward doesn't take it in account.
This is the code, and below, print screens

MEMBERSET[d/Date]=BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YM] ,%P_03_BUD_YEAR% )
MEMBERSET[d/Measures]="LAST_VALUE"
DATA()= 
CARRYFORWARD([d/IL_D_INS_FLOW],"OPENING","ENDING","OPENING"+"PLUG")

thank you 🙂

entry.title: Re: Update on the SAP Identity Management migration to Microsoft Entra
entry.content:
Hi, it is not really comprehensible why SAP decided to choose entra having an existing alternative inhouse. So I hope that we will not be forced to buy a bunch of new licenses and use the SAP products!

The user group is working on that as well!

entry.title: Re: B1Siteuser prompt to reset password to activate b1sit...
entry.content:
Hi,

we face the same situation right now in our environment under SP 2311.
SLD and Extension Manager suddently don't accept the B1SiteUser password anymore.

We did not change any configuration. What is the reason?
I couldn't find an expiry date, neither information that it will lock after too many failed attempts?

Due to bug https://me.sap.com/notes/3463078 we're not able to regain access.
Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: How to export CPQ Logs
entry.content:
One way to achieve this by using existing API to read logs from CPQ and store in whatever tools you prefer.

https://default-us1-prd-01.cpq.cloud.sap/webapihelp/index#/EventLog

entry.title: Read/Write files in ABAP on CLOUD
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are migrating our partner addons from classic ABAP to cloud ABAP. 

In the product , we are getting files / Placing the extracts in cloud SFTP. For these functionalities, we are using CL_HTTP_CLIENT related classes and methods. 
In ABAP on cloud, we couldn't see any released API or similar APIs to read/write the files either from SFTP or application server. 
 
Are there any new processes involved for Read/Write the files in cloud ABAP.

entry.title: Re: Fundamentals of Multitenancy in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Raja,

Good explanation and one can quickly understand the concepts.

I have query,  multitenancy in concept is shared resources, however suggested Hana hdi service for each consumer/tenant. is other dependency services like Audit Log, DPI created separately for each  tenant? what is standard practice? if so loosing concept of shared resource.  is only application (artifacts / runtime ) only shared resource?

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 VS2019 Windows Server 2022 IIS
entry.content:
I am using visual basic aspx files to open the reports


Partial Class myMainAllDevices
Inherits System.Web.UI.Page

Dim myMainAllDevices As New CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument

Protected Sub Page_Init(ByVal sender As Object, ByVal e As System.EventArgs) Handles Me.Init

If Session("Report") Is Nothing Then
Report.Load(Server.MapPath("Report.rpt"))
Session("Report") = Report
Else
myMainAllDevices = CType(Session("myMainAllDevices"), CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument)
End If

Report.Refresh()

Report.SetDatabaseLogon()

With CrystalReportViewer
.DisplayGroupTree = False
.DisplayToolbar = True
.EnableDatabaseLogonPrompt = False
.EnableDrillDown = True
.EnableParameterPrompt = False
.HasCrystalLogo = False
.HasDrillUpButton = False
.HasExportButton = True
.HasGotoPageButton = True
.HasPageNavigationButtons = True
.HasPrintButton = True
.HasRefreshButton = True
.HasSearchButton = True
.HasToggleGroupTreeButton =False
.HasToggleParameterPanelButton = True
.HasZoomFactorList = True
.HyperlinkTarget = "_blank"
.PageZoomFactor = 100
.ReportSource = myMainAllDevices
.ReuseParameterValuesOnRefresh = True
End With

End Sub

End Class

entry.content:

None of the fields on the Canada compliance form are getting auto populated. 

Has anyone faced this issue.

Regards,

Varsha

entry.title: Fiori My Paystubs V3 download button not displaying on Android devices
entry.content:
We are testing the Fiori My Paystubs application V3.  It works fine on pc/laptops.  It works fine using Safari browser on iPhones.  However, on an Android mobile device, the download button is not shown on the payroll list view, so the user is not able to display the PDF.  Any ideas why the download button is missing on Android devices?

Thanks,

Kenneth

entry.title: BPA to excute tcodes in sequence
entry.content:
We are trying to build a POC to execute certain tcodes in sequence in backend S4HANA (On-Prem) via BTP -BPA .

So is this feasible? Any helpful links for similar setup?

Thanks,

Manoj

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE for MARA Characteristic value not replaced
entry.content: If possible , please share your code

entry.title: Re: CDS view to find the Classification characteristic de...
entry.content:
Hi @Gahan_Narayan 

 

The CDS view I_ClfnObjectCharcValForKeyDate displays the characteristic values for an Object  https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_CLFNOBJECTCHARCVALFORKEYDATE

 

A List of Released CDS Views in Classification is available at the resource

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/50af957707fb4c28b6f8b6de940f0a42.html

 

If that doesn't help , could you provide a screenshot of the field you want to get the CDS view for ?

 

Best regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: CDS view to find the Classification characteristic description field for a supplier
entry.content: Adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Research and Development

entry.title: Re: Replacing a wrapped BAPI with a released business object interface
entry.content:
Hi Jelena,

yes, the developer of the BAPI should store the successor information, and the ATC check will find that out and report that there is a BDEF as a successor.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Over-Delivery Qty Tolerance with the customer credit...
entry.content:
Hello, 

In T-Code OVA8 there is a option for Deviation in % (under Released Documents are still unchecked). 

May you can set a percentage here (as per Business requirements) and check. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Standard Report for Billing Item Level
entry.content:
Hi there,

this view has strage location: https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEM.

Alternatively you can create a custom field for the storage location, and copy it on header level. Keep in mind, this will be a split criterion but you should then be able to filter by storage location on the Manage Billing Documents app.

entry.title: Re: Forecast prediction periods - SAC
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

Forecast Feature in widget is currently not available in Optimized stories. It should be coming in a months time in next Q3 QRC update.

In classic stories just check the help page on setup

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/eb08d57f2d964d0b9b75234e481b1ddf.html#procedure

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Everything you need to know about: SAP SuccessFactors Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content:

Thank you, 

-- Robert 🙂

entry.title: Re: Employment Details Portlet Workflow not triggering even though it appears in rule log
entry.content:
Was having the same issue and discovered that it will depend of the scenario.
If the change is done via Take Action the workflow triggers

If the change is done via the Edit button, the workflow does not trigger

entry.title: Re: eInvoice Email-Process - Process Status is stuck on "...
entry.content:
Dear Lukas,

yes, you just send our XML and receive it back in the eDocument Cockpit, validated. Then you can send it via Mail or Peppol, depending on the process.

Having BasicAuthentication in the BTP Destination will definitely be an issue, as this is not supported with SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Backends. S/4 HANA Private Cloud or ECC Backends will work this way, via Cloud Connector.

Regards,

Uwe

entry.title: Re: Download software before the end of maintenance
entry.content: Hi Sanz, Once you are disengaged from SAP Support you are not authorized to download any of the SAP existing software and patches from the SAP support portal, to get further information you may have to contact the SAP account manager in your area.

entry.title: Re: Compliance forms - fields not populated ONB2.0
entry.content: The issue is with the PDF document? or the Actual Compliance Screens?

entry.title: How to extend the P1 support of SAP Data Services 4.2 SP14 P18
entry.content:
What is Mainstream Support and Priority1 Support  for SAP Data Services?

Since we are reaching end of support for SAP DS 4.2 SP14 PATCH18 by the end of December 2024, can we buy extended Priority 1 support for SAP DS 4.2 SP14 PATCH18? 
or do we need to upgrade to the latest version?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to extend the P1 support of SAP Data Services 4.2...
entry.content:
Hi,

For the maintenance phases exact definition, please refer- SAP Maintenance Phases.

If you have specific queries for the extended support, please contact your SAP Account Manager as they have all the information regarding your contract with SAP and can suggest if it is possible to extend your support.

  • ##2785277 - How long is the Extended Support provided for SAP Data Services 4.2?
  • ##2626344 - Who is the SAP Account Manager/Executive for my company?

Thankyou

SS

entry.title: Re: Buenas practicas en SAP para realización de pruebas
entry.content: Muchas gracias por tu ayuda!

entry.title: Valuing dimensions with members # using already available rows
entry.content:
Dear experts,
I would need some help with the development of a Data Action.
After a first step of Cross-Model Copy the row situation is as follows:

ViewEntityMarketProductsCustomerChannelSales CategoryIntercompanyDistr. FlowNet Sales (Local)Std Cost EoL (Local)Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local)
#V10VNProd1DEALERRETCOMMON#STOCK10000––
#V10#Prod1#####–5000

I have taken 2 rows as an example, the purpose would be to go and “merge” the two rows. So with end result the following:

ViewEntityMarketProductsCustomerChannelSales CategoryIntercompanyDistr. FlowNet Sales (Local)Std Cost EoL (Local)Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local)
#V10VNProd1DEALERRETCOMMON#STOCK100005000

Do you have any advice on how this can be done?

Thanks for the help

entry.title: Re: Carry Forward - deletes the first opening month - SAC
entry.content:
@Deb_D 

Use an IF condition to avoid YYYY.01 in the scope from Memberset.

From Help Page documentation

Note that the carry forward function treats the unbooked Closing value for Dec 2018 as 0, so the initial Jan 2019 Opening balance is overwritten. 

In your case 202412 may be 0 or unbooked hence 202501 gets overwritten

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html?q=CARRYFORWARD#carry-forward-function

Just use

IF MONTH([d/Date]) !=1

DATA()= 
CARRYFORWARD([d/IL_D_INS_FLOW],"OPENING","ENDING","OPENING"+"PLUG")

ENDIF

Nikhil

.

entry.title: Re: Quality Defects vs Quality Notification Difference
entry.content:
Hi,

Recording quality defects allows for identifying trends and root causes, while quality notification initiates formal documentation and corrective actions. This structured approach ensures thorough management and resolution of quality issues, leading to improved overall product and process quality. Skipping the step of recording quality defects may result in a less effective approach to resolving quality issues.

Regards

entry.title: Is SAP Business One version 10.0 compatible with Windows 7 ?
entry.content:
Is SAP Business One version 10.0 compatible with Windows 7 ?

I did install SAP B1 10.0 on Windows 7, it worked fine excepted the Crystal Reports where I had an error smi18n.dll missing.

The solution was found on the KBA note
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003378700
and
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/error-upon-preview-cr-layout-the-program-can-t-start-because-smi18n-dll-is/qaq-p/12586547

I cut the C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Common\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64, cut the file secSSO.dll

and now all seems to work fine

entry.title: Re: RFC Metadata in CPI (former HCI)
entry.content: Thanks. It worked for us too

Hello,

if possible deimplement the SAP notes and reimplement the same in the development system.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI to Microsoft SharePoint Adapter Integration
entry.content:
Hello,

From where can I get the ClientID and the Client Secret please

entry.title: How to send an E-Mail Copy when Submitting a Sales Quote
entry.content:
We would like to get a copy of the generated e-mail from C4C.
Especially, the Sales Quote, when the user hits the submit button.
It should be the send message in msg Format, as you normal have it in the sent folder of an e-mail-client.
Is there a way to achieve this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to send an E-Mail Copy when Submitting a Sales Quote
entry.content:
There is no general functionality to send e-mail copies for all types of business documents, but there is an option to send e-mail copies for sales quotes. 

Before you submit a sales quote, you can maintain an e-mail recipient to whom the copy is sent. 

  1. Edit the Sales Quote
  2. Select Actions > Edit Output Settings
  3. Select Send By settings: E-Mail (if not yet maintained) 
  4. In the E-Mail Settings, maintain a row with the 'To' prefix and the E-Mail address of the main recipient (if not yet maintained) 
  5. Select Add Row, maintain the 'CC' prefix and the E-Mail address of the recipient who shall receive a copy
  6. Apply and Save

This is the only option.

A copy of the generated e-mail can only be defined for each individual sales quote.

entry.title: Re: Payment amount in Foreign Currency
entry.content:
I am working on the same problem. The company is based in UK, some workers are based in Ireland. 
In SPRO, I made the changes as shown:

In PR05 the currency can be set to EUR.

But in the overview, it is converted to GBP.

Is there a way to change it?

entry.title: Re: Check the changes in the inventory in chronological o...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

Material Inventories - Balance History

Remove the GL Account  and add Accounting Period / Year and Posting date.
Change the key figures as you need.

the report shows the details from the finacial view so you can also get the opening, In/Out, ending balance as well as quantities.


Regards,

Harshal

entry.title: Automatic job to submit timesheet - S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We would like ot know if there's a way (job, API, etc) to submit timesheet for all employees at a specific moment. 

We would like to configure the system to not release upon saving, but to automatically submit every monday morning all time entries. Is it possible?

We already looked at the API for workforce timesheet but it's not possible to do the requested behavior.

Thank you

SAP Timesheet

entry.title: Re: SAP Code-Jam Request in New York
entry.content: Hello Thomas - I wanted to check in to see if you had a chance to discuss scheduling for December or latest availability as per your team. Looking to hearing from you soon.

entry.title: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Output management in S/4HANA

Introduction to output management

After working with many clients in different fields, I've noticed that most businesses struggle with output management. Each client has their own idea of what output means, whether it's PDF documents, forms, emails, or iDocs. While these views are somewhat correct, I think output management is about effectively communicating with customers or vendors using the right channels and documents.

When we talk about output management, I see it as combining "output" (like print, email, iDoc) with "management" (how we set it up to reuse the functionality). Output management in SAP S/4HANA is a big improvement in how businesses generate and distribute documents. This new framework solves the complexities and inefficiencies of the many old output solutions in previous SAP systems. This blog will mostly cover the output management features in on-premises S/4HANA.

Legacy Output Solutions

Historically, SAP provided several output management tools tailored to specific business areas:

  • SD Output Control (NAST): Used primarily for sales and distribution processes.
  • FI Correspondence: Handles financial document communications.
  • FI-CA Print Workbench: Manages print jobs for contract accounts receivable and payable.
  • CRM Post-Processing Framework (PPF): Deals with post-processing activities in customer relationship management.

While these tools were effective, they required customers to learn and maintain distinct frameworks for each business area. This led to a high total cost of ownership (TCO) and varied user experiences.

What is NAST

NAST is a central component in SAP for managing and controlling the output of documents. It is part of the SAP Output Control framework and is used primarily in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, but its functionality extends to other areas such as Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI).

Key features of NAST include:

  • Message control
  • Output type
  • Condition record
  • Processing routine
  • Transmission medium
  • Scheduling

NAST operates using the condition technique, which is the traditional method of working in SAP.

The Need for a Unified Solution

In SAP S/4HANA projects, the fragmentation of output management tools presented several challenges:

  • Complexity: Customers had to navigate and master different frameworks for different tasks.
  • Maintenance: Each framework required separate setup and ongoing maintenance.
  • User Experience: Disparate tools resulted in inconsistent user experiences across the SAP ecosystem.

What is Output Management in S/4HANA?

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA is a comprehensive framework designed to streamline the creation, formatting, and distribution of business documents. It modernizes and centralizes outdated output mechanisms, effectively meeting the diverse needs of various business processes.

Key features of S/4HANA Output Management include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Offers a single point of configuration for all output types, reducing complexity and easing maintenance. This common setup point can be used across different modules.
  • Flexibility and Scalability: Supports various output channels and formats, making it adaptable to diverse business needs and scalable for future growth.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides consistent and intuitive user interfaces, improving efficiency and satisfaction across different modules.
  • Simplified Maintenance: Eliminates the need for complex master data maintenance and extensive cutover activities.
  • Advanced Output Channels: Supports modern output channels like email, print, fax, and electronic data interchange (EDI), in addition to traditional methods.
  • Improved Template Management: Utilizes advanced tools for designing and managing document templates, such as Adobe Forms, enhancing document consistency and branding.

Different between NAST and Output management in S/4HAN

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

NAST is the traditional output control functionality used in earlier versions of SAP, such as SAP ECC. It manages the output of business documents through various channels like print, email, fax, and EDI. Key characteristics of NAST include:

  • Decentralized Configuration: Each application module (e.g., SD, MM, FI) has its own configuration, leading to complexity and redundancy.
  • Limited Flexibility: Customization and enhancement of output processes require significant effort and often involve custom ABAP development.
  • Condition Records: Utilizes condition records to determine when and how outputs are triggered.
  • Processing Routines: Depends on traditional forms like SAP script and Smart Forms for document formatting.
  • Separate Handling: Each business area (e.g., sales orders, invoices) has its own output determination and processing logic
  • It also covers Multiple Output Mediums and Error Handling and Monitoring

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

The new Output Management framework in SAP S/4HANA is designed to overcome the limitations of NAST and provide a more streamlined and efficient approach. Its key features include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Provides a unified setup for all output types, reducing complexity and maintenance efforts.
  • Enhanced Flexibility: Supports various output channels and formats with easier customization options.
  • Advanced Template Management: Uses Adobe Forms for designing and managing document templates, offering more sophisticated formatting and branding capabilities.
  • Output Parameter Determination: Utilizes a modern method for determining output parameters based on business context, simplifying the output determination process.
  • Improved User Experience: Consistent and user-friendly interfaces across different modules enhance operational efficiency.
  • Integration: Seamlessly integrates with other SAP S/4HANA components and external systems, improving overall document management and processing.

Key Differences

Feature

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Configuration

Decentralized, specific to each module

Centralized, unified for all output types

Flexibility

Limited, requires custom ABAP development

Enhanced, easier customization

Forms and Templates

SAP script, Smart Forms

Adobe Forms

Output Parameter Determination

Condition records

Modern, context-based parameter determination

User Experience

Inconsistent across modules

Consistent and user-friendly

Integration

Separate handling for each business area

Seamless integration with S/4HANA components

Maintenance

Complex, higher total cost of ownership (TCO)

Simplified, lower TCO

 

Advantages and features of Output management

SAP S/4 HANA Output Management has the below features

  • Operates seamlessly in both cloud and on-premises environments.
  • Offers a unified solution for all output related tasks across the product and modules
    Reduce master data maintenance and relatively less time consuming for activation
  • Supports standard extensibility for configuration documents
  • Offers central monitoring tools for key users, enhancing oversight and control.

Based on Business Rule Framework (BRF), offering greater flexibility in managing output processes.

How it works?

For determining output types in transactions, we can utilize Output Parameter Determination (OPD). Maintenance of OPD can function similarly to master data or be set up via transport management. I recommend using OPD as a client-specific change. Using transports can lead to confusion, especially if other rollouts are occurring simultaneously.

Prerequisites –

There are few back-end changes you need to do it before maintenance of OPD setup.

  • Check if your application object type is active
  • Check if your Output type is available and if you want to use your own output then create new one by doing copy of standard output type.
  • Assign channel to your Output type.
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type
  • Assign Email template to output type.

To use Output management, we need to activate the required object and if we activate Output management then system will redirect output always to Output management and not to NAST.

 

Main parameters in OPD (Output Parameter Determination)

Below are the key parameters,

Output management is BRF based functionality which can be setup by using below 7 steps in the SAP using OPD.

Let’s check in the system –

OPD (Output parameter determination) screen

There are multiple steps (as per above screenshot) which should be maintained to determine output in billing document as per this example, here we will take billing output type as an example.

1. Output Type –

This step begins by deciding whether to process the output immediately or later via a job. As shown below, for the combination of billing type F2 and output type BILLING_DOCUMENT, we can choose to process the output type immediately or through a background job (VF31F) at the time of billing document creation. Additionally, in the billing document, we can manually add the output type and set the dispatch time manually.

2. Receiver –

In this step, we can determine the output type for a specific role (partner function). If we have four lines with different roles and want to consider only the first two, we will mark the Exclusive indicator 'X' for the second line. This way, the system will only consider the first two entries and ignore any entries after the second line.

3. Channel –

In this stage, the 'Channel' will be defined. The channel could be IDoc, Print, Email, etc. This stage will also include an exclusive indicator.

For instance, if the exclusive indicator 'X' is set for IDoc as shown below, the system will not search for or determine the PRINT and EMAIL channels.

4. Printer setting –

In this phase, we can specify the printer and the number of copies to be printed after creating the document. Before setting up the printer in this phase, it is necessary to install the printer in SAP.

Additionally, please be aware that the new output management system supports Printer Spool Devices limited to PDF device types only (PDFUC: PDF Unicode 1.3 / PDF1: PDF ISO Latin1 4.60+).

This restriction is detailed under the Symptoms section in note 268480 and SAP Note. 2294198

5. Email setting –

In this phase, the sender's email ID can be configured. This means that when an email is sent to the customer, the sender's email will be set up here, and the recipient's email will be the customer's email address.

Additionally, we can also establish the email template in this phase. An email template specifies the format and content that the customer will receive from us via email.

6. Email recipient –

The email address (recipient address) retrieved in the billing document is sourced from BP master data or determined through this step.

If you have configured an email address here, this setting takes precedence.

Multiple email addresses can be maintained in this determination step by separating them with ';'.
You can also define the email type code with TO, CC, BCC.

7. Form Template –

This step is intended for maintaining the form template used for each output. Ensure that the form template maintained here has been assigned to the output type in SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Form Templates.

If the language is not specified in OPD, it will be retrieved from the customer master data of the output recipient.

8. Output Relevance –

This step verifies if the output item is flagged as relevant for output.

If the relevance indicator is not set to 'X - true', the output item determined in the billing document will not be considered relevant for output.

Consequently, its status will remain 'In preparation' in the billing document under the output section.

OPD functionalities –

Adding extra predefined standard fields

Adding new columns or fields: OPD provides default fields that cover all necessary steps we've discussed. However, often after client discussions, we realize additional parameters are needed to define our objects in OPD differently.

In OPD, there are additional fields available that we can add as columns on the main OPD screen. These can then be utilized in determination combinations as needed.

To do this, navigate to Edit -> Table settings -> Insert column -> From context data object -> Search.

Click on search

Following this step, a list of standard additional fields will be available for us to add and utilize in OPD. If the required field is still not available, we can proceed with an enhancement to add the necessary field to this list. This process requires the involvement of an ABAP specialist.

Simulation functionality

Having reviewed all seven steps, we can use them to determine the output type in the transaction.

To preview the output in simulation mode, use the highlighted option below. This allows us to simulate the output effectively.

Key points and learnings –

Pros

  • As OPD is BRF based functionality, we can import and export data using predefined template.
  • New column (fields) can be added and can be removed as per requirement.
  • SAP S/4HANA output management is recommended but not mandatory.
  • The scope of SAP S/4HANA output management is not comparable with the scope of NAST.
  • SAP S/4HANA output control is not designed to completely replace NAST.
  • Innovations will only be made in the new framework (SAP S/4HANA output management)

Cons –

  • It will only cover the output part of NAST (print, email, partly EDI).
  • The rest – like system integration, workflow, or special function – is not (and will not be) in the scope of SAP S/4HANA output control.
  • Customers will lose all NAST features when switching to SAP S/4HANA Output Control. Using S/4HANA Output Control for applications that previously relied on NAST is only advisable in exceptional cases
  • iDocs are not fully supported by the new output management. There are few restrictions as compared to NAST.
  • NO ALE is supported in Output management.
  • No tool is available for conversion from NAST to Output management.

Reference –

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13492419

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d7bd4b5d70d94f09bcf99b221a7a1688.html

Check out SAP notes related to the topic -

  • SAP Note 2292539 - SAP S/4HANA output control – configuration
  • 2843926 - It is not possible to activate rules in App 'Output Parameter Determination' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Note 2228611 –Output Management in S/4HANA 
  • SAP Note 2248229 –SAP S/4HANA output control - download XML file
  • SAP Note 2292571 –SAP S/4HANA output control -technical setup 
  • SAP Note 2292646 –SAP S/4HANA output control -form templates with fragments 
  • SAP Note 2292681 –SAP S/4HANA output control –master form templates 
  • SAP Note 2294198 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized forms 
  • SAP Note 2367080 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized master forms

entry.title: Re: Task 5 - A "REST" service document (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs&quot
entry.content:
My submission:

The service documents of the REST-Service and the oData-V4 Service are pretty similar. When I try it with cat-service.cds I get a JSON-Response which contains an array with the exposed entities in both cases:

[{"name":"Books","url":"Books"}]

entry.title: Inspection point type for serialized Material
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to take results for inspection lot with Inspection Type 03 for a serialized material. I have created and released a production order, generating the inspection lot. I have used the 320 inspection point type in the routing but when i try to take results, it comes the following message:

"Inspection point type for serialized materials (4) not supported Message no. QT105"

Do you have any information about this error? I would like to use this inspection point type to take different UD per serial number in the same inspection lot.

Thanks in advance,

Lorenzo

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Your first service and first endpoint (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs&qu
entry.content: I'm guessing that's a question ... and if so, you might want to take a look at this part of Capire: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/cds-serve#path. Good luck! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content:
hi @andreag412 ,

to achieve your goal, you would need to. Aggregate data into a variables - the dimensions you want to merge (like Customer, Channel, Sales...), Next you put IF statement to process only rows with Net Sales and overwrite measure values with the aggregated ones. Something like that

VARIABLEMEMBER #Customer OF [d/Customer]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Channel  OF [d/Channel]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Scat 	 OF [d/SalesCat]
VARIABLEMEMBER #IC       OF [d/Intercompany]

DATA([d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
	 [d/Channel] = #Channel,
	 [d/Scat] = #Scat,
	 [d/Intercompany] = #IC) = RESULTLOOKUP()

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="NetSales") != NULL THEN
	DATA([d/Measures] = "StdCost") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measure] = "StdCost"
												  [d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
												  [d/Channel] = #Channel,
												  [d/Scat] = #Scat,
												  [d/Intercompany] = #IC)
	DATA([d/Measures] = "StdCostMark") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measure] = "StdCostMark"
												  [d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
												  [d/Channel] = #Channel,
												  [d/Scat] = #Scat,
												  [d/Intercompany] = #IC)	
ENDIF

 I'm not sure if this code will work, just drafted it. But it can be achieved this way.

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content: one thing, this is not deleting the exisitng row with #, but that I would leave for another discussion

entry.title: How to Import Data from DataBricks Spark on AWS to SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to our comprehensive guide on setting up a JDBC connection between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks. Whether you are using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit or opting for a manual setup, this tutorial will walk you through each step to ensure a smooth and successful integration. By following these detailed instructions, you'll be able to download and install the necessary JDBC drivers, create and configure the required property files, and establish a secure connection to your Databricks SQL warehouses. Let's dive in and get started on enhancing your data analytics capabilities with SAC and Databricks.

Prerequisite

Performed setup using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit or

Performed a manual setup by following the steps 1,2,4,5 in Prerequisites

1. Download and Install JDBC Driver

a. Download the latest JDBC drive from databricks.com All JDBC Driver Versions

b. Extract the zip file and then place the downloaded jar file(s) in an appropriate folder. The jar files can be in any location on your machine as long as the properties file includes the correct file locations. For example, 

2. Create a Property File

a. Create a properties file that specifies the paths to JDBC drivers you want to use, and place it in the same file system where the SAP Analytics Cloud agent is installed. The properties file can have any name, such as DriverConfig.properties. See Example of a properties file

b. Open the file as an administrator and update line from 

#Generic JDBC datasource="path_to_JDBCdriver"

to 

Generic JDBC datasource=C:\Program Files\SAP\SACAgentKit\drivers\DatabricksJDBC42-2.6.36.1062\DatabricksJDBC42.jar

Note: C:\Program Files\SAP\SACAgentKit\drivers\DatabricksJDBC42-2.6.36.1062\DatabricksJDBC42.jar is the path I placed the jar file in the Blog. You have to change it to the real path where you place the jar file in your system

c. Save the property file 

d. Restart Tomcat

3. Find Connection Information in DataBricks

a. JDBC URL

Login DataBricks on AWS > Click SQL Warehouses from the navigational menu> Select SQL warehouses tab > Select a target SQL warehouse name

Click on Connection details tab within the target warehouse > Copy all strings from the JDBC URL box

b. Based on the DataBricks official document, “by default, the connector uses the schema named default and authenticates the connection using the user name token”

c. Based on the DataBricks official document, JDBC Class name is com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver

d. Find password.

Click on the user profile > select User Settings

Select Developer tab from the Settings area > Click on Manage button in the Access tokens section

Click on Generate new token button

Input a meaningful name > set Lifetime days for the token (maximum 10 years) > Click on  Generate > Copy the token string > Store in the secured area (You won’t have chance to check the token value again once you close the prompt window)

4. Build Connection in SAC

a. Go to Connection are in SAC -> Create New Connection -> Search for SQL Database

b. Select correct LocationID and Generic JDBC datasource

Give a meaningful name for the connection (the name can be update later) > Enter JDBC URL > Enter JDBC Class > Enter User Name = token > Enter Password = Access token > (Optional) Select Share credentials when sharing this connection > Click on Create button


Note: If you want to share the credential details, select the option Share these credentials when sharing this connection. Otherwise, users will need to enter their own credentials in order to use the connection. If you don't share your credentials, users will be able to edit their credentials at any time without having to start a data acquisition process.

5. Test Connection

Conclusion

Congratulations on completing the setup of your JDBC connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and Databricks. By following this guide, you have successfully configured your SAC connection to Databricks. With this integration, you can now leverage the powerful analytics and data visualization capabilities of SAC using data stored in Databricks. We hope this guide has been helpful and has made the setup process straightforward and efficient. If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out.

entry.title: Re: Automatic assignment of serial number in outbound deliveries
entry.content: Hello community, any ideas on this?

entry.title: Re: Transport Mexico Einvoice Notes to Quality and Produc...

entry.title: Re: Build Apps + Process Automation Integration Made Easy
entry.content:
Thanks Dan for the detailed videos. This indeed has made integration with SAP Build Apps and Build Process Automation a lot easier. I have two unrelated questions, would be grateful for your insights:-

1. In my app I have all the fields fetching backend S4HANA on-premise data. Using data and variables > Integration I am seeing all the exposed odata services in S4hana system. Some of my fields are Purchase Org, purchase group & company code. Should I create odata services to read each of these customization tables and then use it in the dropdown ? I will be referring your blog Populate Dropdown with SAP Data (ES5) | SAP Tutorials and use the formula  MAP(data.Products1, {label: item.Name, value: item.Id}). Would that be the correct approach to read each of the customization tables from S4HANA on-prem.

2. Could you please refer to any blog/tutorial to create a business object such as Material master and purchase order in a S4HANA on prem system ? 

2.

entry.title: Re: Compliance forms - fields not populated ONB2.0
entry.content: Thank you everyone for reviewing the question asked, I had not enabled Suffix and after enabling it, it worked.

entry.title: Re: MTO Requirements
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

Please note that for Strategy - 82 (Assembly processing with production orders), any changes to the Sales Order schedule line dates get populated to the Production Order during Sales Order item availability check.
But any date (Basic Start/End) changes at the Production Order level does not affect the Sales Order schedule line dates.

 

Can you please check and kindly let me know the trigger at the Production Order level so that the update to the Sales Order schedule line dates is reflected.

 

Thanks,

Kaushik

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Echoflux Web Development Resonance Patterns
entry.content:
Unlocking the Power of Echoflux Web Development Resonance Patterns: A Revolutionary Approach to Harmonizing Web Design and User Experience

Introduction

 

Echoflux Web Development Resonance Patterns (EWDRP) are a new approach to web design and development.

 

EWDRP is essential in today's fast-changing digital world.

 

If you want to optimize your workflow while developing websites, consider checking out our [productivity supplements](your-website-url).

 

This article will explain EWDRP, its principles, benefits, challenges, and practical examples.

 

What is Echoflux?

 

Echoflux combines "echo" and "flux" to symbolize the harmony between web design and user experience.

 

- Echo: Refers to the reflection of user feedback in design.

- Flux: Indicates constant change and adaptability in web development.

 

To enhance your cognitive functions and maintain focus during complex projects, explore our [focus-enhancing supplements](your-website-url).

 

Echoflux represents a new way of understanding web development and user experience, focusing on seamless interaction and continuous improvement.

 

Understanding Resonance Patterns

 

Resonance patterns are crucial in web development for creating an engaging user experience.

 

- Resonance Patterns: These are the consistent and harmonious interactions between various elements of web design and user experience.

 

Traditional approaches to resonance patterns have limitations.

 

- Limitations:

  - Static Design: Fixed design elements that don’t adapt to user feedback.

  - Disjointed User Experience: Inconsistent interactions across different parts of the website.

 

For additional tips on creating engaging web experiences, see our [web design resources](your-website-url).

 

EWDRP introduces a revolutionary approach to resonance patterns, aiming to create a more fluid and engaging web experience.

 

Echoflux Web Development Resonance Patterns (EWDRP): A Deep Dive

 

EWDRP focuses on creating harmony between web design elements and user interactions.

 

core principles and benefits:

 

- Core Principles:

  - User-Centric Design: Prioritizing user needs and feedback in the design process.

  - Adaptive Elements: Design elements that change based on user behavior and preferences.

  - Seamless Interaction: Ensuring smooth transitions and interactions across the website.

 

- Benefits of EWDRP:

  - Enhanced User Experience: Creates a more engaging and intuitive user interface.

  - Improved Web Performance: Optimizes load times and functionality based on user interactions.

  - Increased Design Harmony: Ensures consistent and pleasing design elements throughout the website.

 

Examples and Case Studie

 

1. E-Commerce Websites:

   - Scenario: An online store using EWDRP to adapt product recommendations based on user browsing history.

   - Outcome: Improved user satisfaction and increased sales through personalized shopping experiences.

 

2. Educational Platforms:

   - Scenario: An e-learning platform implementing EWDRP to adjust course content based on student progress and feedback.

   - Outcome: Enhanced learning experience and better student engagement.

 

3. Corporate Websites:

   - Scenario: A business website using EWDRP to dynamically display relevant content based on visitor interests.

   - Outcome: Higher visitor retention and improved customer interaction.

 

Implementing EWDRP: Challenges and Opportunities

 

While EWDRP offers many benefits, implementing it comes with challenges.

 

- Challenges:

  - Technical Complexity: Developing adaptive design elements requires advanced technical skills.

  - Cost: Implementing EWDRP can be costly, especially for small businesses.

  - User Data Privacy: Ensuring user data is collected and used responsibly.

 

Explore how our [supplements](your-website-url) can help you maintain mental clarity when tackling technical complexities.

 

- Opportunities:

  - Innovation: EWDRP opens new possibilities for innovative web design.

  - Competitive Advantage: Websites using EWDRP can stand out in the crowded digital space.

  - Better User Insights: Continuous feedback allows for ongoing improvement and a deeper understanding of user needs.

 

Best Practices for Overcoming Challenges

 

- Invest in Skilled Developers: Ensure your team has the necessary skills to implement EWDRP.

- Start Small: Begin with small, manageable projects to gradually integrate EWDRP.

- Focus on Data Privacy: Implement strong data privacy measures to protect user information.

 

Conclusion

 

Echoflux Web Development Resonance Patterns offer a new way to enhance web design and user experience.

 

By focusing on harmony between design and user interaction, EWDRP can improve web performance and user satisfaction.

 

For more insights on maintaining a high-performance work environment, visit our [supplement solutions](your-website-url).

 

Explore and contribute to the development of EWDRP to shape the future of web development.

entry.title: Re: Routes cannot be mapped to destinations in different...
entry.content: after walking the steps again, and changing the URLs to match my BTP environment (like us30) it worked.

entry.title: Re: New Learning Journey for SAP Master Data Governance for Beginners
entry.content: @Mark63: Thank you for providing the info on different MDG deployment options. I had a query, what if any customer would like to move to Cloud now but the MDG solution "SAP MDG, Cloud edition" provides only Business Partner data model and the MDG solution "SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Edition, MDG" also provides only Business Partner and Product without Central Governance functionality.

entry.title: Re: API for App 'Post unplanned Goods Issue' in S4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hi again,

We have found a solution that Material Document API’s are working with given below payload parameters. 

Stock Correction with 711 from E-WM

 

  • 711: GI InvDiff.:whouse

 

{

    "PostingDate": "2024-07-01T10:00:00",

    "GoodsMovementCode": "03",

    "CtrlPostgForExtWhseMgmtSyst":"3",

    "ReferenceDocument":"SRCNFKB",

    "to_MaterialDocumentItem": [{

        "Plant": "1210",

        "StorageLocation": "125W",

        "Batch": "",

        "EWMWarehouse": "1250",

        "EWMStorageBin": "S001-01-01",

        "Material": "TG11",

        "GoodsMovementType": "711",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "3",

        "GoodsMovementReasonCode":"0001"

    }

  ]

}

 

Material Document: 4900000757 (Starter Tenant)

 

Stock Correction with 712 from E-WM

 

  • 712: GR InvDiff.:whouse

{

    "PostingDate": "2024-07-01T10:00:00",

    "GoodsMovementCode": "03",

    "CtrlPostgForExtWhseMgmtSyst":"3",

    "ReferenceDocument":"SRCNFKB",

    "to_MaterialDocumentItem": [{

        "Plant": "1210",

        "StorageLocation": "125W",

        "Batch": "",

        "EWMWarehouse": "1250",

        "EWMStorageBin": "S001-01-01",

        "Material": "TG11",

        "GoodsMovementType": "712",

        "QuantityInEntryUnit": "1",

        "GoodsMovementReasonCode":"0001"

    }

  ]

}

 

Material Document: 4900000758 (Starter Tenant)

 

Fahrettin

entry.title: Me salvó la timidez
entry.content:
Hace un tiempo me preguntaron esto:

cual es tu mayor debilidad? Me quede pasmada por un segundo, pero había leído que cuando eso pasa debes respirar 5 segundos para que el oxigeno llegue a tu cerebro y te tranquilices, entonces lo  hice y una brillante idea llego a mi:

Dije ; "Mi mayor debilidad es la timidez, soy muy tímida"  el entrevistador me miró asombrado y yo agregue,

" Pero empecé un curso hace un mes para vencer la timidez y hablar en publico y me ha funcionado de maravilla, míreme aquí sentada frente a usted con toda la tranquilidad aplicando buenas tácticas para hablar en público"

Les cuento que llevo 16 años trabajando aquí, liderando procesos de SAP y por supuesto hablando en público de maravilla!

Saludos!

entry.title: How to display full screen picture in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am developing a mobile app using MDK.

I have to display several pictures from the backend.

I have managed to implement the OData interface for that.

The picture is displayed in the ImageCollection in the SectionedTable using online OData request:

"Controls": [
    {
      "Sections": [
         {
          "ImageCell": {
            "Image": "/SAPAssetManager/Services/OnlineAssetManager.service/{@odata.readLink}/$value",          
            "Title": "{@odata.readLink}"
          },
          "Layout": {
            "LayoutType": "Vertical",
            "NumberOfColumns": 1
          },  
          "Target": {
            "EntitySet": "ZPictures",
            "Service": "/SAPAssetManager/Services/OnlineAssetManager.service",
            "QueryOptions": "$filter=ZZDocNr eq '{ZZDocNr}' and ZZPicNr eq '{ZZPicNr}'"
          },        
          "_Type": "Section.Type.ImageCollection"
        }
      ],
      "LoadingIndicator": {
        "Enabled": true,
        "Text": "$(L,z_details_loading)"
      },
      "_Name": "PictureSecTab",
      "_Type": "Control.Type.SectionedTable"
    }
  ]
 
But the problem is, that the picture is very small (see attachment) and I would like to have it full screen.
How can I do it in a standard way?
Is there another component to display the picture?
Or can I somehow provide the image size?
 
Many thanks,
Chris.

entry.title: Activate Draft instance on POST call
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Have built a Managed RAP with draft mode. I am doing a POST service call with Payloads, and it should create entries under the Persistent Custom table. But the issue is it creates entries in Log table and sometimes it doesn't.

Have gone through link Preparing Draft Instances for Activation | SAP Help Portal but I am not sure where I should call below Activate logic so it can activate the draft instances and it can be moved to Persistent Table.  

Below is the Business definition logic placed.

Please guide me how It can be achieved. 

Br,

entry.title: Where can I start learning the billing and invoicing process?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Contract Accounting & Invoicing learning journey

Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the generic billing, invoicing and accounting processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Contract Accounting and Invoicing, within the context of SAP Solutions for Quote to Cash Management public cloud (BRIM and CPQ).

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to execute the billing and invoicing process and gain an overview of the Revenue Sharing and Partner Settlement. In addition, you will learn to describe and execute the integration of Billing and Invoicing with Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable and General Ledger Accounting. The use of both transactional and, most importantly analytical Fiori Apps for Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting is covered.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud top interest in segmentation
entry.content:
Hello @SCHNEIDERT,
May I suggest below flow which will suffice your use-case:

  1. Create & Configure Scoring Rules & Profiles.
  2. Calculate Scores as per your example usecase
  3. Create Segments using the score filter to determine Top Interest (You might need to do custom logic to determine highest score maybe!)
  4. ready to send personalised and targeted emails.

If this doesn't work, we don't have any alternative as per my experience!
I hope this answers your query to close the thread.

Thanks and Regards,
Kunal Bansal
SAP CX Solution Architect

entry.title: Re: No payload deserializer available for resource kind '...
entry.content:
I faced the same issue when i was trying to call the api (action) from ".http" extension file,

the issue got resolved when i updated the request header from  content-type : 'application/json' 

to content-type : application/json.

It would look something like this

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Acquiring Core ABAP Skills - example files can not reached
entry.content:
Dear Jeannot,

Not really. I have opened 2 tickets for this problem. My first ticket just got closed and I do reply for the second one. It says that this software is not supported by that group.

This problem appears a lot of times during lessons. And my 90 days BTP trial is just starting to run out. 

I am at loss now.

Best Regards

Máté Pál

entry.title: SAP Business One MT messages
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As you aware MT messages are no longer be supported by SWIFT.
We would like to know in future B1 will also support MX format or it will have some internal conversion?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Tip: SAP GUI Scripting VBA Code Snippet to Detect all IDs of the UI Elements
entry.content:
Hola qurm

Intente acomodar el codigo, pero me sale error, por favor compartir código con la mejora, gracias.

I tried to adjust the code, but I got an error, please share code with the improvement, thank you.

entry.title: Re: Can we use existing AFO reports in SAC? If yes then w...
entry.content:
Hello @ADI ,

As Steffen mentioned in his blog, you can export the AFO reports to SAC tenant and view them from there.   Kindly refer to his blog below:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-analysis-for-microsoft-office-storage-of-workbooks/ba-p/13558032

Regards,
Dhyanesh

entry.title: SAP Marketing Cloud - Configuring Forms in Content Studio with dependencies
entry.content:
Hello SAP community

If we are using a form in SAP Content Studio, we don't see any possibility to hide the following (input)field in a form, depending on the chosen value of the previous one.

Example: IF Country is chosen USA, than show field Region, otherwise hide it.

Is this per design or are we missing something important here? We thought we may can work around it while enhancing with css, but also did not succeed.

The only work-around we see at the moment is to work with restricted values on the following field (what would work as long as these two fields have a data dependency).

Many thanks for a good hint!

Best regards, Stefan

entry.title: Re: how to manage different custom mappings for same docu...
entry.content:
While performing Custom Mapping we have a provision to provide system ID for which the mapping applies to. You can leverage this option to maintain different mapping for ECC and S4.

In case you have mapping for ECC alone, mention the system ID of ECC while performing custom mapping and deploy it. 

In actual run and based on custom routing, system ID is determined and the custom mapping performed for that specific system ID will be utilized.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: Issue with posting asset transactions during migration process
entry.content: Jan-Niklas Studt is right, change the transfer status NOT the company code status. The details are list on the help portal https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a185c3d89dae4ffc85723f27f624944a.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Adding new tabs in Manage Purchase Requisitions Professional
entry.content:
Need custom tab to display table information in Fiori app F2229. Please suggest recommended approach.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Adding new tabs in Manage Purchase Requisitions Profe...
entry.content:
Hello customer, 

To add a new section, please follow this documentation: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/a357047be956436ebb1dfebf1aa29af2.html

You cannot add a section using 'Custom Fields & Logic app' or a BAdI. You need to add a fragment extension.
It's recommended that you create a custom app if you want to add your customization. In case of any bugs, it would be easier to check in the future if they are caused by SAP standard code or your own code. In addition, you may need to adapt your custom code each time you upgrade the system.

Adding only custom fields via 'Custom Fields & Logic app' is the recommended approach. 

Best regards, 
Tatiana

entry.title: Re: Issue in creating document using SAP BTP DMS API'S from CAPM nodejs project
entry.content: Hi Jigar Salecha, I am also facing similar issue , Did you get chance to get this issue fixed , if yes could you please share the solution.

entry.title: Subject: How to Prevent Tables from Loading into Memory in SAP HANA
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am looking for ways to improve memory performance in SAP HANA by preventing certain tables from being loaded into memory. I have already set the UNLOAD PRIORITY to 0 for these tables, which helps, but I have noticed that HANA occasionally still loads these tables into memory.

Here are my specific questions:

  1. Are there any other methods or configurations in SAP HANA to ensure that certain tables are not loaded into memory under any circumstances?
  2. Is there a way to monitor and control table load behavior more effectively?
  3. Can specific users or processes be restricted from loading certain tables into memory?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Can SAP PAPM run on SAP Datasphere Only?
entry.content: SAP PaPM Cloud can even run for customers who do not have SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content: Thanks for the help, I have implemented this logic: But we don't get the desired result, because the table shows:

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content: View Entity Market Products Customer Channel Sales Category Intercompany Distr. Flow Net Sales (Local) Std Cost EoL (Local) Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local) # V10 VN Prod1 DEALER RET COMMON # STOCK 10000 – – # V10 # Prod1 # # # # # 500 500 500

entry.title: Re: How to schedule a SAP Certification exam on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am having a problem: when I fail an exam and try to rebook it, there is no option such as schedule available.

Can please somebody advise what I can do to sort it out

 

Stella

entry.title: Re: SSO Cert Update
entry.content: Hi @rmellish - Multiple SSO certificates can be stored at the same time. After confirming the new SSO certificate works successfully, the old one can be removed.

entry.title: Re: How can I remove "Manager" as a recipient of a specific Completed Document Email Notif
entry.content: "Employee Only" is not an option you can select from the Recipients drop down list. If this is important to you, please submit an enhancement idea if one is not already out there, and then post about it, with a link, to the SAP Community to get more votes. Platform Influence: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/86

entry.title: Good Day Seniors,
entry.content: Please help  me with the Below error , i have valid legal ID , though getting the below error while importing the foundation data "Location" , please help help , i tried all the formats like Text , General, Number formats in CSV file but still getting the same error .

entry.title: Batch operator script engine service layer sap b1
entry.content:
In service layer SAP b1 have support batch with b1s/v1/$batch. but I want implement it in script engine SAP B1.

please help me and suggestions any idea for this case. 
@Trinidad @ANKIT_CHAUHAN  please help it.

thanks you

entry.title: Re: How to display full screen picture in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
The Image Collection is designed to show a thumbnail view of an image along with optional labels.

If you want to display an image to fill the width of the page you should consider using the Image Section which will give you options on how large the image will render.  However if you need multiple images you will need an image section per image to be displayed.

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content:
HI @MichalCK,

Thanks for the help, I have implemented this logic:
 

 

VARIABLEMEMBER #Customer OF [d/Customer]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Channel OF [d/Channel]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Scat OF [d/SalesCateg]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Market OF [d/Market]
VARIABLEMEMBER #DF OF [d/DistributionFlow]

DATA([d/Customer] = #Customer,
	 [d/Channel] = #Channel,
	 [d/SalesCateg] = #Scat,
	 [d/DistributionFlow] = #DF,
	 [d/Market] = #Market) = RESULTLOOKUP()

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "NET_SALES_LOCAL") != NULL THEN
	DATA([d/Measures] = "STD_COST_EOL_LOCAL") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Customer] = #Customer,
															 [d/Channel] = #Channel,
															 [d/SalesCateg] = #Scat,
															 [d/DistributionFlow] = #DF,
															 [d/Market] = #Market,
															 [d/Measures] = "STD_COST_EOL_LOCAL")
	DATA([d/Measures] = "STD_COST_MKUP_REV_LOCAL") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Customer] = #Customer,
																  [d/Channel] = #Channel,
																  [d/SalesCateg] = #Scat,
																  [d/DistributionFlow] = #DF,
																  [d/Market] = #Market,
																  [d/Measures] = "STD_COST_MKUP_REV_LOCAL")
ENDIF

 

 
But we don't get the desired result, because the table shows:
 
ViewEntityMarketProductsCustomerChannelSales CategoryIntercompanyDistr. FlowNet Sales (Local)Std Cost EoL (Local)Std Cost Mark Up Reverse (Local)
#V10VNProd1DEALERRETCOMMON#STOCK10000––
#V10#Prod1#####500500500

entry.title: Re: Me salvó la timidez
entry.content: Yes, it's great you turned a weakness / liability into a strength.  Well done!

entry.title: Re: Converting a SAP Fiori “Manage” app to a Display Only using Adapt UI
entry.content:
Thanks Colleen. This is the exact issue we are facing with, as the security model in SAP GUI doesn't work anymore in Fiori.

The Enterprise Search is not really a good user experience, imo. Fiori Launchpad is supposed to show users exactly what they need to do for their job. It would be bad usability to expect users to use tiles for some tasks, and for other things remember to do Searches.

Adapt UI and creating runtime variants seems to be the only way to provide display-only version of apps, without breaking the user experience principles of Fiori framework.

Thanks
